title: Laws of Motion
subtitle: Chapter 4: Laws of Motion — NEB Class 11

content.text: Dynamics is the branch of mechanics that deals with the motion of objects and the forces causing that motion.
Kinematics: Study of motion without considering forces. Dynamics: Study of motion with forces.
A force is a push or pull that can change the state of rest or motion of a body. SI unit: Newton (N).
Types of forces: Contact forces (friction, tension, normal force) and Non-contact forces (gravitational, electric, magnetic).
First Law (Law of Inertia): A body remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.
Inertia: The tendency of a body to resist change in its state.
Second Law: The rate of change of momentum is directly proportional to the applied force.

content.name: Newton's Second Law
content.note: F = force, m = mass, a = acceleration.

content.text: Third Law: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.

content.name: Momentum Formula
content.note: p = momentum, m = mass, v = velocity. Momentum is a vector quantity. SI unit: kg·m/s.

content.text: In an isolated system, total momentum remains constant. Examples: collisions and explosions.
Friction: Opposes motion between surfaces in contact (Static and Kinetic). Tension: Force transmitted through a string or rope. Normal Reaction: Force exerted by a surface perpendicular to it.
Motion in a circular path with constant speed. It requires a centripetal force directed towards the center.

content.name: Centripetal Force
content.note: F = centripetal force, m = mass, v = velocity, r = radius.
